question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /network/stake REST endpoint with concurrent requests to exhaust the 5-second timeout window, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to always resolve to Status.DOWN and permanently report the network as unable to confirm transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a TimeoutException in restNetworkStakeHealth() by saturating the network path to the REST API, causing the onErrorResume branch to set status to Status.DOWN and falsely signal a total network shutdown to all health consumers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a ConnectException by exhausting TCP connection pools to the REST API host, causing the rootCause instanceof ConnectException branch to set Status.DOWN and suppress legitimate UP health signals, masking the network's actual ability to confirm transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the short-circuit logic in health() — where restNetworkStakeHealth() returning non-UP skips publishing() and subscribing() entirely — to permanently suppress transaction-confirmation health checks by keeping the REST stake endpoint returning 5xx errors?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror-node--039/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CLUSTER_UP to be set to 0 by repeatedly triggering health() failures, causing downstream alerting systems to treat the network as shut down and halt transaction submission even when the network is operational?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all TransactionGenerator scenarios() to report a rate of 0.0 by flooding the publish pipeline, causing publishing() to emit a DOWN/UNKNOWN health signal and triggering a false total-network-shutdown alert?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all MirrorSubscriber subscriptions to report getRate() == 0 by disrupting the gRPC/REST subscription streams, causing subscribing() to fall through to getHealthForZeroRate() and report DOWN, falsely indicating a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getHealthForZeroRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the failWhenInactive=false default in ReleaseHealthProperties to cause getHealthForZeroRate() to return UNKNOWN instead of DOWN, masking a real total network shutdown by making the health status appear ambiguous rather than critical?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a 503 response from the /network/stake endpoint (e.g., via cache poisoning or DNS spoofing) to cause statusCode.is5xxServerError() to return true, setting Status.DOWN and triggering automated shutdown procedures based on a false health signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the REST API to return a 4xx response (e.g., 429 Too Many Requests via rate-limit exhaustion), causing statusCode.is5xxServerError() to be false and status to be set to Status.UNKNOWN, hiding a real network shutdown behind an ambiguous health state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive chain in health() — specifically the flatMap short-circuit when restNetworkStakeHealth() is not UP — to prevent subscribing() from ever being evaluated, causing the monitor to never detect that subscriptions are still active and the network is still confirming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: registerGauge()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger shared across all instances to cause a race condition in registerGauge(), resulting in the Micrometer gauge reporting stale or incorrect health values that mask a network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent health() calls to cause a race between multiple recordHealthMetric() invocations on the static CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger, resulting in a transient 0 value that triggers false shutdown alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of retry logic in restNetworkStakeHealth() — a single transient network error immediately resolves to DOWN — to cause a momentary disruption to be treated as a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed 5-second timeout in restNetworkStakeHealth() by introducing exactly 5 seconds of latency on the /network/stake endpoint, causing every health check to time out and permanently report Status.DOWN?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() to return an empty Flux by disrupting all subscriber connections, causing subscribing() to call switchIfEmpty(getHealthForZeroRate()) and report DOWN, falsely indicating the network cannot confirm transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transactionGenerator.scenarios() to emit an empty Flux, causing the reduce() in publishing() to produce 0.0 and trigger getHealthForZeroRate(), resulting in a false DOWN signal that halts transaction confirmation monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the switchIfEmpty(subscribing()) chain in health() to cause a scenario where publishing() emits empty (all rates > 0) but subscribing() reports DOWN, resulting in a false total-shutdown signal when transactions are actually being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS poisoning attack against the mirror node REST API hostname to redirect /network/stake requests to a controlled server returning 500, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to permanently report Status.DOWN and trigger a false network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message format in String.format('Network stake status is %s with error: %s', status, e.getMessage()) to inject log-forging content via a crafted error message from a spoofed REST endpoint, causing misleading shutdown log entries?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause ExceptionUtils.getRootCause(e) to return an unexpected exception type (not ConnectException or TimeoutException) by triggering an SSL handshake failure, causing status to remain UNKNOWN and hide a real network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause Scenario.getRate() to return exactly 0.0 for all subscriptions by flooding the subscription pipeline with no-op messages, causing the filter(sum -> sum > 0) to drop the result and subscribing() to report DOWN?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause Scenario.getRate() to return exactly 0.0 for all publish scenarios by starving the publish pipeline, causing the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) in publishing() to pass and trigger a false DOWN health signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Mono composition in health() to cause a subscription cancellation mid-chain, resulting in doOnNext(this::recordHealthMetric) never being called and CLUSTER_UP remaining stale at 0, falsely indicating a network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a null health object in recordHealthMetric() by causing the reactive chain to emit null, causing the null-check fallback to Status.UP to incorrectly set CLUSTER_UP=1 and mask a real network shutdown?
